calculation.method: reported close and daily move from the AP closing-index ledger; daily moves are versus the prior regular session of 2026-06-22

text: US stocks fell broadly on Tuesday, June 23, 2026, led by technology. The S&P 500 fell 107.33 points (1.4%) to 7,365.46; the Nasdaq Composite fell 579.56 points (2.2%) to 25,587.04; the Dow Jones Industrial Average, which carries less technology weighting, fell 45.87 points (0.1%) to 51,666.84; and the Russell 2000 fell 28.92 points (about 1%) to 2,975.48.

subject.event: fell back below 3,000 one session after first-ever close above it

calculation.method: today's AP close minus the 2026-06-22 close (3,004.40) equals -28.92 points; the index closed back below the 3,000 level it had cleared for the first time the prior session

text: The small-cap Russell 2000 fell 28.92 points (about 1%) to 2,975.48, slipping back below 3,000 just one session after its first-ever close above that level (3,004.40) on June 22. The milestone was given back as the broad risk-off tech selloff spread.

value.basis: Micron is the AP reported close; the other chip percentages are intraday snapshots from TheStreet's live blog and may differ from final closes

market_session: US regular session on 2026-06-23 (Micron close; other chip moves intraday)
text: Semiconductors led the decline. Micron Technology, which reports earnings the next day, lost 13.2% per the AP ledger. Intraday on TheStreet's live blog, Qualcomm fell about 8.6%, Arm Holdings about 8.4%, Taiwan Semiconductor about 5.2%, SanDisk about 11.7%, and Nvidia about 2.7%; those intraday figures are carried at medium confidence and may differ from final closes.

value.kospi_note: steepest drop in more than three months
value.trading_halt: automatic 20-minute halt triggered on the Korea exchange
value.catalyst: regulatory signals that the chip rally had become overheated (Reuters, cited by TheStreet)

market_session: Asian session on 2026-06-23 ahead of the US open
text: The selloff originated overseas. South Korea's Kospi plunged 9.99%, its steepest drop in more than three months, after regulators cautioned that the chip rally had become overheated; Samsung Electronics and SK Hynix each tumbled more than 12%, triggering an automatic 20-minute trading halt. That rout fed directly into US semiconductor and AI-linked names.

value.theme: AI trade cooling / questions over overstretched AI valuations
value.proximate_trigger: Broadcom results reportedly failed to deliver the guidance boost markets expected, per Hargreaves Lansdown's Anna Macdonald, triggering a US semiconductor selloff that fed into Asia
value.record_context: the AI-led rally had carried indexes to record highs into early June

text: The proximate driver was AI-trade fatigue. Yahoo Finance framed the day as the AI trade cooling off with semiconductors retreating, and TheStreet cited Hargreaves Lansdown strategist Anna Macdonald attributing the move to Broadcom's results failing to deliver the guidance boost markets expected, which sparked a US chip selloff that fed into Asia overnight. The AP noted the selling was concentrated in AI-related companies that had run up to record after record, most recently in early June.

value.bofa_note: Bank of America research note flagged no rate cuts until 2028 and as many as three rate hikes this year
value.ap_framing: investors worried the growing likelihood of rate hikes this year to fight inflation will weigh on economic growth
value.us2y_note: 2-year yield hit its highest since February 2025
value.oil: oil prices fell even as yields stayed elevated

text: A macro overhang amplified the selling. TheStreet highlighted a Bank of America note portending no rate cuts until 2028 and up to three rate hikes this year, and the AP framed the day around worries that a growing likelihood of rate hikes to fight inflation will weigh on growth. The 10-year Treasury yield sat around 4.495% and the 2-year reached its highest since February 2025, even as oil prices fell. Yield and BofA figures are intraday and carried at medium confidence.

subject.topic: narrow tech-led decline with broader internals firmer

value.basis: single-stock figures are intraday or premarket snapshots from TheStreet's live blog

market_session: US regular session on 2026-06-23 (single-stock figures intraday/premarket)
text: The decline was narrow: TheStreet reported the S&P 500's technology sector down about 4.1% even as roughly 299 of the index's 500 holdings traded higher, and the Dow's lighter tech weighting limited it to a 0.1% loss. Defensive and software names plus some mega-cap tech bucked the slide, with Microsoft up about 2.5% and Amazon up about 1.7% intraday, and IBM (+4.2%), Accenture (+3.3%) and Public Storage (+4.4%) higher premarket. These single-stock figures are intraday or premarket snapshots and are carried at medium confidence.
